revision.text:
{{infobox Onsen |Name = Neko-naki Onsen |image = 猫啼温泉郷看板 2012.jpg |Comment = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300}} |Location = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]][[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District]][[:en:Ishikawa, Fukushima|Ishikawa Town]] |Access = By car: from [[:en:Abukuma Kōgen Road|Abukuma Kogen Road]] and {{ill|Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|de|Tamagawa Anschlussstelle (Fukushima)|ja|玉川インターチェンジ (福島県)|lt=Tamagawa IC|lt_ja=玉川IC|lt_de=Tamagawa IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|qq=}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]] for approximately 20 minutes, and from [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|de|Sukagawa Anschlussstelle|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|lt=Sukagawa IC|lt_ja=須賀川IC|lt_de=Sukagawa IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]] for approximately 40 minutes.
By train: approximately a 15‑minute walk from [[:en:Suigun Line|Mizugun Line]] [[:en:Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|Ishikawa Station]]. |Spring Quality = {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|de|Radioaktive Quelle|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} |Temperature = 8°C |Discharge = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Guest Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = |Special Notes = }} '''Neko-naki Onsen''' (猫啼温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District]], [[:en:Ishikawa, Fukushima|Ishikawa Town]] (formerly part of the old [[:en:Provinces of Japan|State]] [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]; and since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era, part of the old [[:en:Iwaki Province (1868)|Iwaki Province]]). == Water Quality == * Simple weak radioactive cold mineral spring ** [[:en:Quell|Spring Mouth]][[:en:Temperature|Temperature]]8.0°C == Hot Spring Area == Although there is no [[:en:Spa town|Onsen town]], there are two [[:en:Ryokan|Inn]]s located in the basin of {{ill|Imadegawa (Fukushima)|de|Imadegawa (Fukushima)|ja|今出川 (福島県)|lt=Imadegawa|lt_ja=今出川|lt_de=Imadegawa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imadegawa (Fukushima)|qq=}}. Novels set in the local inns have also been written, such as [[:en:Yasuo Uchida|Yasuo Uchida]]’s “Thirteen Tombstones” and [[:en:Funahashi Seiichi|Seiichi Funahashi]]’s “A Certain Woman’s Distant View”. == History == This hot spring is said to be associated with the mid‑Heian period female {{ill|Poet|de|Dichter|ja|歌人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poet|qq=}}, [[:en:Izumi Shikibu|Izumi Shikibu]]. According to the {{ill|Legend of the Spring’s Opening|de|Legende der Quelle|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Legend of the Spring’s Opening|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, when Izumi Shikibu was en route to Kyoto, she left her [[:en:Cat|Beloved Cat]] behind. It is said that the abandoned cat continued to cry in search of its master, Izumi Shikibu. Consequently, the hot spring came to be known as Neko‑naki Onsen. Later, although the cat fell ill, it regained its vitality after soaking in the hot spring. This discovery of the mineral spring’s healing properties by the locals led to the development of the area as a spa resort. == Access == * By car: approximately 20 minutes via [[:en:Abukuma Kōgen Road|Abukuma Kogen Road]] and {{ill|Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|de|Tamagawa Anschlussstelle (Fukushima)|ja|玉川インターチェンジ (福島県)|lt=Tamagawa IC|lt_ja=玉川IC|lt_de=Tamagawa IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|qq=}} from [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]], and approximately 40 minutes via [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|de|Sukagawa Anschlussstelle|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|lt=Sukagawa IC|lt_ja=須賀川IC|lt_de=Sukagawa IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=}} from [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]]. * By train: approximately a 15‑minute walk from [[:en:Suigun Line|Mizugun Line]] [[:en:Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|Ishikawa Station]]. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Ishikawa Town ** {{ill|Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|de|Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|ja|片倉温泉 (福島県)|lt=Katakura Onsen|lt_ja=片倉温泉|lt_de=Katakura Onsen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|qq=}} ** {{ill|Haha-bata Onsen|de|Haha-bata Onsen|ja|母畑温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haha-bata Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|de|Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|ja|塩ノ沢温泉 (福島県)|lt=Shionosawa Onsen|lt_ja=塩ノ沢温泉|lt_de=Shionosawa Onsen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://navi.town.ishikawa.fukushima.jp/blog/entry/003989.html Tour the four hot spring resorts of Ishikawa Town and enjoy the renowned “spa treatment” experience.] - Ishikawa Town * [https://www.minyu-net.com/serial/f-onsen/FM20180429-265897.php Fukushima Onsen Exploration: Neko‑naki Onsen] - {{ill|Fukushima Minyu Newspaper|de|Fukushima Minyu Zeitung|ja|福島民友新聞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Minyu Newspaper|qq=}} {{Onsen-stub}} Nekodaki Onsen is a radioactive spring {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|猫啼温泉|version=100778076|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Hot Springs]] [[Category:Ishikawa Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Radioactive springs]] }} [[ja:猫啼温泉]] [[de:Katzenweinen Onsen]] [[sn:Nekodaki Onsen]] [[Category:Izumi Shikibu]] [[Category:Ishikawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:Hot springs of Fukushima Prefecture]]

By train: approximately a 15‑minute walk from [[:en:Suigun Line|Mizugun Line]] [[:en:Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|Ishikawa Station]]. |Spring Quality = {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|de|Radioaktive Quelle|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} |Temperature = 8°C |Discharge = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Guest Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = |Special Notes = }} '''Neko-naki Onsen''' (猫啼温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District]], [[:en:Ishikawa, Fukushima|Ishikawa Town]] (formerly part of the old [[:en:Provinces of Japan|State]] [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]; and since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era, part of the old [[:en:Iwaki Province (1868)|Iwaki Province]]). == Water Quality == * Simple weak radioactive cold mineral spring ** [[:en:Quell|Spring Mouth]][[:en:Temperature|Temperature]]8.0°C == Hot Spring Area == Although there is no [[:en:Spa town|Onsen town]], there are two [[:en:Ryokan|Inn]]s located in the basin of {{ill|Imadegawa (Fukushima)|de|Imadegawa (Fukushima)|ja|今出川 (福島県)|lt=Imadegawa|lt_ja=今出川|lt_de=Imadegawa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imadegawa (Fukushima)|qq=}}. Novels set in the local inns have also been written, such as [[:en:Yasuo Uchida|Yasuo Uchida]]’s “Thirteen Tombstones” and [[:en:Funahashi Seiichi|Seiichi Funahashi]]’s “A Certain Woman’s Distant View”. == History == This hot spring is said to be associated with the mid‑Heian period female {{ill|Poet|de|Dichter|ja|歌人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poet|qq=}}, [[:en:Izumi Shikibu|Izumi Shikibu]]. According to the {{ill|Legend of the Spring’s Opening|de|Legende der Quelle|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Legend of the Spring’s Opening|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, when Izumi Shikibu was en route to Kyoto, she left her [[:en:Cat|Beloved Cat]] behind. It is said that the abandoned cat continued to cry in search of its master, Izumi Shikibu. Consequently, the hot spring came to be known as Neko‑naki Onsen. Later, although the cat fell ill, it regained its vitality after soaking in the hot spring. This discovery of the mineral spring’s healing properties by the locals led to the development of the area as a spa resort. == Access == * By car: approximately 20 minutes via [[:en:Abukuma Kōgen Road|Abukuma Kogen Road]] and {{ill|Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|de|Tamagawa Anschlussstelle (Fukushima)|ja|玉川インターチェンジ (福島県)|lt=Tamagawa IC|lt_ja=玉川IC|lt_de=Tamagawa IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|qq=}} from [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]], and approximately 40 minutes via [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|de|Sukagawa Anschlussstelle|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|lt=Sukagawa IC|lt_ja=須賀川IC|lt_de=Sukagawa IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=}} from [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]]. * By train: approximately a 15‑minute walk from [[:en:Suigun Line|Mizugun Line]] [[:en:Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|Ishikawa Station]]. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Ishikawa Town ** {{ill|Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|de|Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|ja|片倉温泉 (福島県)|lt=Katakura Onsen|lt_ja=片倉温泉|lt_de=Katakura Onsen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|qq=}} ** {{ill|Haha-bata Onsen|de|Haha-bata Onsen|ja|母畑温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haha-bata Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|de|Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|ja|塩ノ沢温泉 (福島県)|lt=Shionosawa Onsen|lt_ja=塩ノ沢温泉|lt_de=Shionosawa Onsen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://navi.town.ishikawa.fukushima.jp/blog/entry/003989.html Tour the four hot spring resorts of Ishikawa Town and enjoy the renowned “spa treatment” experience.] - This discovery of the mineral spring’s healing properties by the locals led to the development of the area as a spa resort. == Access == * By car: approximately 20 minutes via [[:en:Abukuma Kōgen Road|Abukuma Kogen Road]] and {{ill|Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|de|Tamagawa Anschlussstelle (Fukushima)|ja|玉川インターチェンジ (福島県)|lt=Tamagawa IC|lt_ja=玉川IC|lt_de=Tamagawa IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|qq=}} from [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]], and approximately 40 minutes via [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|de|Sukagawa Anschlussstelle|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|lt=Sukagawa IC|lt_ja=須賀川IC|lt_de=Sukagawa IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=}} from [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]]. * By train: approximately a 15‑minute walk from [[:en:Suigun Line|Mizugun Line]] [[:en:Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|Ishikawa Station]]. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Ishikawa Town ** {{ill|Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|de|Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|ja|片倉温泉 (福島県)|lt=Katakura Onsen|lt_ja=片倉温泉|lt_de=Katakura Onsen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|qq=}} ** {{ill|Haha-bata Onsen|de|Haha-bata Onsen|ja|母畑温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haha-bata Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|de|Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|ja|塩ノ沢温泉 (福島県)|lt=Shionosawa Onsen|lt_ja=塩ノ沢温泉|lt_de=Shionosawa Onsen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://navi.town.ishikawa.fukushima.jp/blog/entry/003989.html Tour the four hot spring resorts of Ishikawa Town and enjoy the renowned “spa treatment” experience.] - Ishikawa Town * [https://www.minyu-net.com/serial/f-onsen/FM20180429-265897.php Fukushima Onsen Exploration: Neko‑naki Onsen] - {{ill|Fukushima Minyu Newspaper|de|Fukushima Minyu Zeitung|ja|福島民友新聞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Minyu Newspaper|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福島民友}} {{Onsen-stub}} Nekodaki Onsen is a radioactive spring {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|猫啼温泉|version=100778076|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ishikawa Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Radioactive springs]] }} [[ja:猫啼温泉]] [[de:Katzenweinen Onsen]] [[sn:Nekodaki Onsen]] [[Category:Izumi Shikibu]] [[Category:Ishikawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:Hot springs of Fukushima Prefecture]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Ishikawa Town
revision.text:
{{infobox Onsen |Name = Neko-naki Onsen |image = 猫啼温泉郷看板 2012.jpg |Comment = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300}} |Location = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]][[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District]][[:en:Ishikawa, Fukushima|Ishikawa Town]] |Access = By car: from [[:en:Abukuma Kōgen Road|Abukuma Kogen Road]] and {{ill|Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|de|Tamagawa Anschlussstelle (Fukushima)|ja|玉川インターチェンジ (福島県)|lt=Tamagawa IC|lt_ja=玉川IC|lt_de=Tamagawa IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|qq=}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]] for approximately 20 minutes, and from [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|de|Sukagawa Anschlussstelle|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|lt=Sukagawa IC|lt_ja=須賀川IC|lt_de=Sukagawa IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]] for approximately 40 minutes.
By train: approximately a 15‑minute walk from [[:en:Suigun Line|Mizugun Line]] [[:en:Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|Ishikawa Station]]. |Spring Quality = {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|de|Radioaktive Quelle|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} |Temperature = 8°C |Discharge = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Guest Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = |Special Notes = }} '''Neko-naki Onsen''' (猫啼温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District]], [[:en:Ishikawa, Fukushima|Ishikawa Town]] (formerly part of the old [[:en:Provinces of Japan|State]] [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]; and since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era, part of the old [[:en:Iwaki Province (1868)|Iwaki Province]]). == Water Quality == * Simple weak radioactive cold mineral spring ** [[:en:Quell|Spring Mouth]][[:en:Temperature|Temperature]]8.0°C == Hot Spring Area == Although there is no [[:en:Spa town|Onsen town]], there are two [[:en:Ryokan|Inn]]s located in the basin of {{ill|Imadegawa (Fukushima)|de|Imadegawa (Fukushima)|ja|今出川 (福島県)|lt=Imadegawa|lt_ja=今出川|lt_de=Imadegawa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imadegawa (Fukushima)|qq=}}. Novels set in the local inns have also been written, such as [[:en:Yasuo Uchida|Yasuo Uchida]]’s “Thirteen Tombstones” and [[:en:Funahashi Seiichi|Seiichi Funahashi]]’s “A Certain Woman’s Distant View”. == History == This hot spring is said to be associated with the mid‑Heian period female {{ill|Poet|de|Dichter|ja|歌人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poet|qq=}}, [[:en:Izumi Shikibu|Izumi Shikibu]]. According to the {{ill|Legend of the Spring’s Opening|de|Legende der Quelle|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Legend of the Spring’s Opening|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, when Izumi Shikibu was en route to Kyoto, she left her [[:en:Cat|Beloved Cat]] behind. It is said that the abandoned cat continued to cry in search of its master, Izumi Shikibu. Consequently, the hot spring came to be known as Neko‑naki Onsen. Later, although the cat fell ill, it regained its vitality after soaking in the hot spring. This discovery of the mineral spring’s healing properties by the locals led to the development of the area as a spa resort. == Access == * By car: approximately 20 minutes via [[:en:Abukuma Kōgen Road|Abukuma Kogen Road]] and {{ill|Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|de|Tamagawa Anschlussstelle (Fukushima)|ja|玉川インターチェンジ (福島県)|lt=Tamagawa IC|lt_ja=玉川IC|lt_de=Tamagawa IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|qq=}} from [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]], and approximately 40 minutes via [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|de|Sukagawa Anschlussstelle|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|lt=Sukagawa IC|lt_ja=須賀川IC|lt_de=Sukagawa IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=}} from [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]]. * By train: approximately a 15‑minute walk from [[:en:Suigun Line|Mizugun Line]] [[:en:Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|Ishikawa Station]]. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Ishikawa Town ** {{ill|Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|de|Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|ja|片倉温泉 (福島県)|lt=Katakura Onsen|lt_ja=片倉温泉|lt_de=Katakura Onsen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|qq=}} ** {{ill|Haha-bata Onsen|de|Haha-bata Onsen|ja|母畑温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haha-bata Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|de|Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|ja|塩ノ沢温泉 (福島県)|lt=Shionosawa Onsen|lt_ja=塩ノ沢温泉|lt_de=Shionosawa Onsen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://navi.town.ishikawa.fukushima.jp/blog/entry/003989.html Tour the four hot spring resorts of Ishikawa Town and enjoy the renowned “spa treatment” experience.] - Ishikawa Town * [https://www.minyu-net.com/serial/f-onsen/FM20180429-265897.php Fukushima Onsen Exploration: Neko‑naki Onsen] - {{ill|Fukushima Minyu Newspaper|de|Fukushima Minyu Zeitung|ja|福島民友新聞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Minyu Newspaper|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福島民友}} {{Onsen-stub}} Nekodaki Onsen is a radioactive spring {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|猫啼温泉|version=100778076|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Radioactive springs]] }} [[ja:猫啼温泉]] [[de:Katzenweinen Onsen]] [[sn:Nekodaki Onsen]] [[Category:Izumi Shikibu]] [[Category:Ishikawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:Hot springs of Fukushima Prefecture]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11572001]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Neko-naki Onsen |image = 猫啼温泉郷看板 2012.jpg |Comment = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300}} |Location = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]][[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District]][[:en:Ishikawa, Fukushima|Ishikawa Town]] |Access = By car: from [[:en:Abukuma Kōgen Road|Abukuma Kogen Road]] and {{ill|Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|de|Tamagawa Anschlussstelle (Fukushima)|ja|玉川インターチェンジ (福島県)|lt=Tamagawa IC|lt_ja=玉川IC|lt_de=Tamagawa IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|qq=|1=Tamagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572547}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]] for approximately 20 minutes, and from [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|de|Sukagawa Anschlussstelle|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|lt=Sukagawa IC|lt_ja=須賀川IC|lt_de=Sukagawa IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]] for approximately 40 minutes.
By train: approximately a 15‑minute walk from [[:en:Suigun Line|Mizugun Line]] [[:en:Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|Ishikawa Station]]. |Spring Quality = {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|de|Radioaktive Quelle|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} |Temperature = 8°C |Discharge = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Guest Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = |Special Notes = }} '''Neko-naki Onsen''' (猫啼温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District]], [[:en:Ishikawa, Fukushima|Ishikawa Town]] (formerly part of the old [[:en:Provinces of Japan|State]] [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]; and since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era, part of the old [[:en:Iwaki Province (1868)|Iwaki Province]]). == Water Quality == * Simple weak radioactive cold mineral spring ** [[:en:Quell|Spring Mouth]][[:en:Temperature|Temperature]]8.0°C == Hot Spring Area == Although there is no [[:en:Spa town|Onsen town]], there are two [[:en:Ryokan|Inn]]s located in the basin of {{ill|Imadegawa (Fukushima)|de|Imadegawa (Fukushima)|ja|今出川 (福島県)|lt=Imadegawa|lt_ja=今出川|lt_de=Imadegawa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imadegawa (Fukushima)|qq=|1=Imade River (Fukushima)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q83237244}}. Novels set in the local inns have also been written, such as [[:en:Yasuo Uchida|Yasuo Uchida]]’s “Thirteen Tombstones” and [[:en:Funahashi Seiichi|Seiichi Funahashi]]’s “A Certain Woman’s Distant View”. == History == This hot spring is said to be associated with the mid‑Heian period female {{ill|Poet|de|Dichter|ja|歌人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poet|qq=|1=waka poet|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11122954}}, [[:en:Izumi Shikibu|Izumi Shikibu]]. According to the {{ill|Legend of the Spring’s Opening|de|Legende der Quelle|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Legend of the Spring’s Opening|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, when Izumi Shikibu was en route to Kyoto, she left her [[:en:Cat|Beloved Cat]] behind. It is said that the abandoned cat continued to cry in search of its master, Izumi Shikibu. Consequently, the hot spring came to be known as Neko‑naki Onsen. Later, although the cat fell ill, it regained its vitality after soaking in the hot spring. This discovery of the mineral spring’s healing properties by the locals led to the development of the area as a spa resort. == Access == * By car: approximately 20 minutes via [[:en:Abukuma Kōgen Road|Abukuma Kogen Road]] and {{ill|Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|de|Tamagawa Anschlussstelle (Fukushima)|ja|玉川インターチェンジ (福島県)|lt=Tamagawa IC|lt_ja=玉川IC|lt_de=Tamagawa IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|qq=|1=Tamagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572547}} from [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]], and approximately 40 minutes via [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|de|Sukagawa Anschlussstelle|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|lt=Sukagawa IC|lt_ja=須賀川IC|lt_de=Sukagawa IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} from [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]]. * By train: approximately a 15‑minute walk from [[:en:Suigun Line|Mizugun Line]] [[:en:Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|Ishikawa Station]]. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Ishikawa Town ** {{ill|Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|de|Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|ja|片倉温泉 (福島県)|lt=Katakura Onsen|lt_ja=片倉温泉|lt_de=Katakura Onsen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakura Onsen {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11572001]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Neko-naki Onsen |image = 猫啼温泉郷看板 2012.jpg |Comment = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300}} |Location = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]][[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District]][[:en:Ishikawa, Fukushima|Ishikawa Town]] |Access = By car: from [[:en:Abukuma Kōgen Road|Abukuma Kogen Road]] and {{ill|Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|de|Tamagawa Anschlussstelle (Fukushima)|ja|玉川インターチェンジ (福島県)|lt=Tamagawa IC|lt_ja=玉川IC|lt_de=Tamagawa IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|qq=|1=Tamagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572547}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]] for approximately 20 minutes, and from [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|de|Sukagawa Anschlussstelle|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|lt=Sukagawa IC|lt_ja=須賀川IC|lt_de=Sukagawa IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]] for approximately 40 minutes.
By train: approximately a 15‑minute walk from [[:en:Suigun Line|Mizugun Line]] [[:en:Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|Ishikawa Station]]. |Spring Quality = {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|de|Radioaktive Quelle|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} |Temperature = 8°C |Discharge = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Guest Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = |Special Notes = }} '''Neko-naki Onsen''' (猫啼温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District]], [[:en:Ishikawa, Fukushima|Ishikawa Town]] (formerly part of the old [[:en:Provinces of Japan|State]] [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]; and since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era, part of the old [[:en:Iwaki Province (1868)|Iwaki Province]]). == Water Quality == * Simple weak radioactive cold mineral spring ** [[:en:Quell|Spring Mouth]][[:en:Temperature|Temperature]]8.0°C == Hot Spring Area == Although there is no [[:en:Spa town|Onsen town]], there are two [[:en:Ryokan|Inn]]s located in the basin of {{ill|Imadegawa (Fukushima)|de|Imadegawa (Fukushima)|ja|今出川 (福島県)|lt=Imadegawa|lt_ja=今出川|lt_de=Imadegawa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imadegawa (Fukushima)|qq=|1=Imade River (Fukushima)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q83237244}}. Novels set in the local inns have also been written, such as [[:en:Yasuo Uchida|Yasuo Uchida]]’s “Thirteen Tombstones” and [[:en:Funahashi Seiichi|Seiichi Funahashi]]’s “A Certain Woman’s Distant View”. == History == This hot spring is said to be associated with the mid‑Heian period female {{ill|Poet|de|Dichter|ja|歌人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poet|qq=|1=waka poet|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11122954}}, [[:en:Izumi Shikibu|Izumi Shikibu]]. According to the {{ill|Legend of the Spring’s Opening|de|Legende der Quelle|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Legend of the Spring’s Opening|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, when Izumi Shikibu was en route to Kyoto, she left her [[:en:Cat|Beloved Cat]] behind. It is said that the abandoned cat continued to cry in search of its master, Izumi Shikibu. Consequently, the hot spring came to be known as Neko‑naki Onsen. Later, although the cat fell ill, it regained its vitality after soaking in the hot spring. This discovery of the mineral spring’s healing properties by the locals led to the development of the area as a spa resort. == Access == * By car: approximately 20 minutes via [[:en:Abukuma Kōgen Road|Abukuma Kogen Road]] and {{ill|Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|de|Tamagawa Anschlussstelle (Fukushima)|ja|玉川インターチェンジ (福島県)|lt=Tamagawa IC|lt_ja=玉川IC|lt_de=Tamagawa IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|qq=|1=Tamagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572547}} from [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]], and approximately 40 minutes via [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|de|Sukagawa Anschlussstelle|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|lt=Sukagawa IC|lt_ja=須賀川IC|lt_de=Sukagawa IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} from [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]]. * By train: approximately a 15‑minute walk from [[:en:Suigun Line|Mizugun Line]] [[:en:Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|Ishikawa Station]]. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Ishikawa Town ** {{ill|Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|de|Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|ja|片倉温泉 (福島県)|lt=Katakura Onsen|lt_ja=片倉温泉|lt_de=Katakura Onsen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|qq=|1=Katakura Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569535}} ** {{ill|Haha-bata Onsen|de|Haha-bata Onsen|ja|母畑温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haha-bata Onsen|qq=|1=Bobata Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547236}} ** {{ill|Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|de|Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|ja|塩ノ沢温泉 (福島県)|lt=Shionosawa Onsen|lt_ja=塩ノ沢温泉|lt_de=Shionosawa Onsen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://navi.town.ishikawa.fukushima.jp/blog/entry/003989.html Tour the four hot spring resorts of Ishikawa Town and enjoy the renowned “spa treatment” experience.] - Ishikawa Town * [https://www.minyu-net.com/serial/f-onsen/FM20180429-265897.php Fukushima Onsen Exploration: Neko‑naki Onsen] - {{ill|Fukushima Minyu Newspaper|de|Fukushima Minyu Zeitung|ja|福島民友新聞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Minyu Newspaper|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福島民友|1=Fukushima Minyu Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593163}} {{Onsen-stub}} Nekodaki Onsen is a radioactive spring {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|猫啼温泉|version=100778076|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Radioactive spring]] }} [[ja:猫啼温泉]] [[ja:猫啼温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572001]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Neko-naki Onsen |image = 猫啼温泉郷看板 2012.jpg |Comment = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300}} |Location = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]][[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District]][[:en:Ishikawa, Fukushima|Ishikawa Town]] |Access = By car: from [[:en:Abukuma Kōgen Road|Abukuma Kogen Road]] and {{ill|Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|de|Tamagawa Anschlussstelle (Fukushima)|ja|玉川インターチェンジ (福島県)|lt=Tamagawa IC|lt_ja=玉川IC|lt_de=Tamagawa IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|qq=|1=Tamagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572547}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]] for approximately 20 minutes, and from [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|de|Sukagawa Anschlussstelle|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|lt=Sukagawa IC|lt_ja=須賀川IC|lt_de=Sukagawa IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]] for approximately 40 minutes.
By train: approximately a 15‑minute walk from [[:en:Suigun Line|Mizugun Line]] [[:en:Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|Ishikawa Station]]. |Spring Quality = {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|de|Radioaktive Quelle|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} |Temperature = 8°C |Discharge = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Guest Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = |Special Notes = }} '''Neko-naki Onsen''' (猫啼温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District]], [[:en:Ishikawa, Fukushima|Ishikawa Town]] (formerly part of the old [[:en:Provinces of Japan|State]] [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]; and since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era, part of the old [[:en:Iwaki Province (1868)|Iwaki Province]]). == Water Quality == * Simple weak radioactive cold mineral spring ** [[:en:Quell|Spring Mouth]][[:en:Temperature|Temperature]]8.0°C == Hot Spring Area == Although there is no [[:en:Spa town|Onsen town]], there are two [[:en:Ryokan|Inn]]s located in the basin of {{ill|Imadegawa (Fukushima)|de|Imadegawa (Fukushima)|ja|今出川 (福島県)|lt=Imadegawa|lt_ja=今出川|lt_de=Imadegawa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imadegawa (Fukushima)|qq=|1=Imade River (Fukushima)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q83237244}}. Novels set in the local inns have also been written, such as [[:en:Yasuo Uchida|Yasuo Uchida]]’s “Thirteen Tombstones” and [[:en:Funahashi Seiichi|Seiichi Funahashi]]’s “A Certain Woman’s Distant View”. == History == This hot spring is said to be associated with the mid‑Heian period female {{ill|Poet|de|Dichter|ja|歌人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poet|qq=|1=waka poet|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11122954}}, [[:en:Izumi Shikibu|Izumi Shikibu]]. According to the {{ill|Legend of the Spring’s Opening|de|Legende der Quelle|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Legend of the Spring’s Opening|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, when Izumi Shikibu was en route to Kyoto, she left her [[:en:Cat|Beloved Cat]] behind. It is said that the abandoned cat continued to cry in search of its master, Izumi Shikibu. Consequently, the hot spring came to be known as Neko‑naki Onsen. Later, although the cat fell ill, it regained its vitality after soaking in the hot spring. This discovery of the mineral spring’s healing properties by the locals led to the development of the area as a spa resort. == Access == * By car: approximately 20 minutes via [[:en:Abukuma Kōgen Road|Abukuma Kogen Road]] and {{ill|Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|de|Tamagawa Anschlussstelle (Fukushima)|ja|玉川インターチェンジ (福島県)|lt=Tamagawa IC|lt_ja=玉川IC|lt_de=Tamagawa IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|qq=|1=Tamagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572547}} from [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]], and approximately 40 minutes via [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|de|Sukagawa Anschlussstelle|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|lt=Sukagawa IC|lt_ja=須賀川IC|lt_de=Sukagawa IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} from [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]]. * By train: approximately a 15‑minute walk from [[:en:Suigun Line|Mizugun Line]] [[:en:Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|Ishikawa Station]]. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Ishikawa Town ** {{ill|Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|de|Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|ja|片倉温泉 (福島県)|lt=Katakura Onsen|lt_ja=片倉温泉|lt_de=Katakura Onsen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|qq=|1=Katakura Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569535}} ** {{ill|Haha-bata Onsen|de|Haha-bata Onsen|ja|母畑温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haha-bata Onsen|qq=|1=Bobata Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547236}} ** {{ill|Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|de|Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|ja|塩ノ沢温泉 (福島県)|lt=Shionosawa Onsen|lt_ja=塩ノ沢温泉|lt_de=Shionosawa Onsen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://navi.town.ishikawa.fukushima.jp/blog/entry/003989.html Tour the four hot spring resorts of Ishikawa Town and enjoy the renowned “spa treatment” experience.] - Ishikawa Town * [https://www.minyu-net.com/serial/f-onsen/FM20180429-265897.php Fukushima Onsen Exploration: Neko‑naki Onsen] - {{ill|Fukushima Minyu Newspaper|de|Fukushima Minyu Zeitung|ja|福島民友新聞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Minyu Newspaper|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福島民友|1=Fukushima Minyu Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593163}} {{Onsen-stub}} Nekodaki Onsen is a radioactive spring {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|猫啼温泉|version=100778076|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Radioactive spring]] }} [[ja:猫啼温泉]] revision.text:
[[ja:猫啼温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572001]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Neko-naki Onsen |image = 猫啼温泉郷看板 2012.jpg |Comment = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300}} |Location = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]][[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District]][[:en:Ishikawa, Fukushima|Ishikawa Town]] |Access = By car: from [[:en:Abukuma Kōgen Road|Abukuma Kogen Road]] and {{ill|Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|de|Tamagawa Anschlussstelle (Fukushima)|ja|玉川インターチェンジ (福島県)|lt=Tamagawa IC|lt_ja=玉川IC|lt_de=Tamagawa IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|1=Tamagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572547}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]] for approximately 20 minutes, and from [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|de|Sukagawa Anschlussstelle|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|lt=Sukagawa IC|lt_ja=須賀川IC|lt_de=Sukagawa IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]] for approximately 40 minutes.
By train: approximately a 15‑minute walk from [[:en:Suigun Line|Mizugun Line]] [[:en:Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|Ishikawa Station]]. |Spring Quality = {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|de|Radioaktive Quelle|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} |Temperature = 8°C |Discharge = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Guest Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = |Special Notes = }} '''Neko-naki Onsen''' (猫啼温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District]], [[:en:Ishikawa, Fukushima|Ishikawa Town]] (formerly part of the old [[:en:Provinces of Japan|State]] [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]; and since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era, part of the old [[:en:Iwaki Province (1868)|Iwaki Province]]). == Water Quality == * Simple weak radioactive cold mineral spring ** [[:en:Quell|Spring Mouth]][[:en:Temperature|Temperature]]8.0°C == Hot Spring Area == Although there is no [[:en:Spa town|Onsen town]], there are two [[:en:Ryokan|Inn]]s located in the basin of {{ill|Imadegawa (Fukushima)|de|Imadegawa (Fukushima)|ja|今出川 (福島県)|lt=Imadegawa|lt_ja=今出川|lt_de=Imadegawa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imadegawa (Fukushima)|1=Imade River (Fukushima)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q83237244}}. Novels set in the local inns have also been written, such as [[:en:Yasuo Uchida|Yasuo Uchida]]’s “Thirteen Tombstones” and [[:en:Funahashi Seiichi|Seiichi Funahashi]]’s “A Certain Woman’s Distant View”. == History == This hot spring is said to be associated with the mid‑Heian period female {{ill|Poet|de|Dichter|ja|歌人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poet|1=waka poet|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11122954}}, [[:en:Izumi Shikibu|Izumi Shikibu]]. According to the {{ill|Legend of the Spring’s Opening|de|Legende der Quelle|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Legend of the Spring’s Opening|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, when Izumi Shikibu was en route to Kyoto, she left her [[:en:Cat|Beloved Cat]] behind. It is said that the abandoned cat continued to cry in search of its master, Izumi Shikibu. Consequently, the hot spring came to be known as Neko‑naki Onsen. Later, although the cat fell ill, it regained its vitality after soaking in the hot spring. This discovery of the mineral spring’s healing properties by the locals led to the development of the area as a spa resort. == Access == * By car: approximately 20 minutes via [[:en:Abukuma Kōgen Road|Abukuma Kogen Road]] and {{ill|Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|de|Tamagawa Anschlussstelle (Fukushima)|ja|玉川インターチェンジ (福島県)|lt=Tamagawa IC|lt_ja=玉川IC|lt_de=Tamagawa IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|1=Tamagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572547}} from [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]], and approximately 40 minutes via [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|de|Sukagawa Anschlussstelle|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|lt=Sukagawa IC|lt_ja=須賀川IC|lt_de=Sukagawa IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} from [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]]. * By train: approximately a 15‑minute walk from [[:en:Suigun Line|Mizugun Line]] [[:en:Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|Ishikawa Station]]. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Ishikawa Town ** {{ill|Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|de|Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|ja|片倉温泉 (福島県)|lt=Katakura Onsen|lt_ja=片倉温泉|lt_de=Katakura Onsen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|1=Katakura Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569535}} ** {{ill|Haha-bata Onsen|de|Haha-bata Onsen|ja|母畑温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haha-bata Onsen|1=Bobata Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547236}} ** {{ill|Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|de|Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|ja|塩ノ沢温泉 (福島県)|lt=Shionosawa Onsen|lt_ja=塩ノ沢温泉|lt_de=Shionosawa Onsen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://navi.town.ishikawa.fukushima.jp/blog/entry/003989.html Tour the four hot spring resorts of Ishikawa Town and enjoy the renowned “spa treatment” experience.] - Ishikawa Town * [https://www.minyu-net.com/serial/f-onsen/FM20180429-265897.php Fukushima Onsen Exploration: Neko‑naki Onsen] - {{ill|Fukushima Minyu Newspaper|de|Fukushima Minyu Zeitung|ja|福島民友新聞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Minyu Newspaper|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福島民友|1=Fukushima Minyu Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593163}} {{Onsen-stub}} Nekodaki Onsen is a radioactive spring {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|猫啼温泉|version=100778076|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Radioactive spring]] }} [[ja:猫啼温泉]] [[de:Katzenweinen Onsen]] [[Category:Izumi Shikibu]] [[Category:Ishikawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:Hot springs of Fukushima Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11572001}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:猫啼温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572001]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Neko-naki Onsen |image = 猫啼温泉郷看板 2012.jpg |Comment = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300}} |Location = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]][[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District]][[:en:Ishikawa, Fukushima|Ishikawa Town]] |Access = By car: from [[:en:Abukuma Kōgen Road|Abukuma Kogen Road]] and {{ill|Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|de|Tamagawa Anschlussstelle (Fukushima)|ja|玉川インターチェンジ (福島県)|lt=Tamagawa IC|lt_ja=玉川IC|lt_de=Tamagawa IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|1=Tamagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572547}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]] for approximately 20 minutes, and from [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|de|Sukagawa Anschlussstelle|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|lt=Sukagawa IC|lt_ja=須賀川IC|lt_de=Sukagawa IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]] for approximately 40 minutes.
By train: approximately a 15‑minute walk from [[:en:Suigun Line|Mizugun Line]] [[:en:Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|Ishikawa Station]]. |Spring Quality = {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|de|Radioaktive Quelle|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} |Temperature = 8°C |Discharge = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Guest Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = |Special Notes = }} '''Neko-naki Onsen''' (猫啼温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District]], [[:en:Ishikawa, Fukushima|Ishikawa Town]] (formerly part of the old [[:en:Provinces of Japan|State]] [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]; and since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era, part of the old [[:en:Iwaki Province (1868)|Iwaki Province]]). == Water Quality == * Simple weak radioactive cold mineral spring ** [[:en:Quell|Spring Mouth]][[:en:Temperature|Temperature]]8.0°C == Hot Spring Area == Although there is no [[:en:Spa town|Onsen town]], there are two [[:en:Ryokan|Inn]]s located in the basin of {{ill|Imadegawa (Fukushima)|de|Imadegawa (Fukushima)|ja|今出川 (福島県)|lt=Imadegawa|lt_ja=今出川|lt_de=Imadegawa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imadegawa (Fukushima)|1=Imade River (Fukushima)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q83237244}}. Novels set in the local inns have also been written, such as [[:en:Yasuo Uchida|Yasuo Uchida]]’s “Thirteen Tombstones” and [[:en:Funahashi Seiichi|Seiichi Funahashi]]’s “A Certain Woman’s Distant View”. == History == This hot spring is said to be associated with the mid‑Heian period female {{ill|Poet|de|Dichter|ja|歌人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poet|1=waka poet|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11122954}}, [[:en:Izumi Shikibu|Izumi Shikibu]]. According to the {{ill|Legend of the Spring’s Opening|de|Legende der Quelle|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Legend of the Spring’s Opening|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, when Izumi Shikibu was en route to Kyoto, she left her [[:en:Cat|Beloved Cat]] behind. It is said that the abandoned cat continued to cry in search of its master, Izumi Shikibu. Consequently, the hot spring came to be known as Neko‑naki Onsen. Later, although the cat fell ill, it regained its vitality after soaking in the hot spring. This discovery of the mineral spring’s healing properties by the locals led to the development of the area as a spa resort. == Access == * By car: approximately 20 minutes via [[:en:Abukuma Kōgen Road|Abukuma Kogen Road]] and {{ill|Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|de|Tamagawa Anschlussstelle (Fukushima)|ja|玉川インターチェンジ (福島県)|lt=Tamagawa IC|lt_ja=玉川IC|lt_de=Tamagawa IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|1=Tamagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572547}} from [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]], and approximately 40 minutes via [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|de|Sukagawa Anschlussstelle|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|lt=Sukagawa IC|lt_ja=須賀川IC|lt_de=Sukagawa IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} from [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]]. * By train: approximately a 15‑minute walk from [[:en:Suigun Line|Mizugun Line]] [[:en:Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|Ishikawa Station]]. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Ishikawa Town ** {{ill|Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|de|Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|ja|片倉温泉 (福島県)|lt=Katakura Onsen|lt_ja=片倉温泉|lt_de=Katakura Onsen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|1=Katakura Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569535}} ** {{ill|Haha-bata Onsen|de|Haha-bata Onsen|ja|母畑温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haha-bata Onsen|1=Bobata Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547236}} ** {{ill|Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|de|Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|ja|塩ノ沢温泉 (福島県)|lt=Shionosawa Onsen|lt_ja=塩ノ沢温泉|lt_de=Shionosawa Onsen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://navi.town.ishikawa.fukushima.jp/blog/entry/003989.html Tour the four hot spring resorts of Ishikawa Town and enjoy the renowned “spa treatment” experience.] - Ishikawa Town * [https://www.minyu-net.com/serial/f-onsen/FM20180429-265897.php Fukushima Onsen Exploration: Neko‑naki Onsen] - {{ill|Fukushima Minyu Newspaper|de|Fukushima Minyu Zeitung|ja|福島民友新聞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Minyu Newspaper|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福島民友|1=Fukushima Minyu Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593163}} {{Onsen-stub}} Nekodaki Onsen is a radioactive spring {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|猫啼温泉|version=100778076|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Radioactive spring]] }} [[ja:猫啼温泉]] [[de:Katzenweinen Onsen]] [[Category:Izumi Shikibu]] [[Category:Ishikawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:Hot springs of Fukushima Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11572001}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:猫啼温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572001]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Neko-naki Onsen |image = 猫啼温泉郷看板 2012.jpg |Comment = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300}} |Location = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]][[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District]][[:en:Ishikawa, Fukushima|Ishikawa Town]] |Access = By car: from [[:en:Abukuma Kōgen Road|Abukuma Kogen Road]] and {{ill|Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|de|Tamagawa Anschlussstelle (Fukushima)|ja|玉川インターチェンジ (福島県)|lt=Tamagawa IC|lt_ja=玉川IC|lt_de=Tamagawa IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|1=Tamagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572547}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]] for approximately 20 minutes, and from [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|de|Sukagawa Anschlussstelle|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|lt=Sukagawa IC|lt_ja=須賀川IC|lt_de=Sukagawa IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]] for approximately 40 minutes.
By train: approximately a 15‑minute walk from [[:en:Suigun Line|Mizugun Line]] [[:en:Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|Ishikawa Station]]. |Spring Quality = {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|de|Radioaktive Quelle|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} |Temperature = 8°C |Discharge = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Guest Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = |Special Notes = }} '''Neko-naki Onsen''' (猫啼温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District]], [[:en:Ishikawa, Fukushima|Ishikawa Town]] (formerly part of the old [[:en:Provinces of Japan|State]] [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]; and since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era, part of the old [[:en:Iwaki Province (1868)|Iwaki Province]]). == Water Quality == * Simple weak radioactive cold mineral spring ** [[:en:Quell|Spring Mouth]][[:en:Temperature|Temperature]]8.0°C == Hot Spring Area == Although there is no [[:en:Spa town|Onsen town]], there are two [[:en:Ryokan|Inn]]s located in the basin of {{ill|Imadegawa (Fukushima)|de|Imadegawa (Fukushima)|ja|今出川 (福島県)|lt=Imadegawa|lt_ja=今出川|lt_de=Imadegawa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imadegawa (Fukushima)|1=Imade River (Fukushima)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q83237244}}. Novels set in the local inns have also been written, such as [[:en:Yasuo Uchida|Yasuo Uchida]]’s “Thirteen Tombstones” and [[:en:Funahashi Seiichi|Seiichi Funahashi]]’s “A Certain Woman’s Distant View”. == History == This hot spring is said to be associated with the mid‑Heian period female {{ill|Poet|de|Dichter|ja|歌人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poet|1=waka poet|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11122954}}, [[:en:Izumi Shikibu|Izumi Shikibu]]. According to the {{ill|Legend of the Spring’s Opening|de|Legende der Quelle|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Legend of the Spring’s Opening|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, when Izumi Shikibu was en route to Kyoto, she left her [[:en:Cat|Beloved Cat]] behind. It is said that the abandoned cat continued to cry in search of its master, Izumi Shikibu. Consequently, the hot spring came to be known as Neko‑naki Onsen. Later, although the cat fell ill, it regained its vitality after soaking in the hot spring. This discovery of the mineral spring’s healing properties by the locals led to the development of the area as a spa resort. == Access == * By car: approximately 20 minutes via [[:en:Abukuma Kōgen Road|Abukuma Kogen Road]] and {{ill|Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|de|Tamagawa Anschlussstelle (Fukushima)|ja|玉川インターチェンジ (福島県)|lt=Tamagawa IC|lt_ja=玉川IC|lt_de=Tamagawa IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|1=Tamagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572547}} from [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]], and approximately 40 minutes via [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|de|Sukagawa Anschlussstelle|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|lt=Sukagawa IC|lt_ja=須賀川IC|lt_de=Sukagawa IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} from [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]]. * By train: approximately a 15‑minute walk from [[:en:Suigun Line|Mizugun Line]] [[:en:Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|Ishikawa Station]]. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Ishikawa Town ** {{ill|Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|de|Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|ja|片倉温泉 (福島県)|lt=Katakura Onsen|lt_ja=片倉温泉|lt_de=Katakura Onsen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|1=Katakura Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569535}} ** {{ill|Haha-bata Onsen|de|Haha-bata Onsen|ja|母畑温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haha-bata Onsen|1=Bobata Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547236}} ** {{ill|Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|de|Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|ja|塩ノ沢温泉 (福島県)|lt=Shionosawa Onsen|lt_ja=塩ノ沢温泉|lt_de=Shionosawa Onsen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://navi.town.ishikawa.fukushima.jp/blog/entry/003989.html Tour the four hot spring resorts of Ishikawa Town and enjoy the renowned “spa treatment” experience.] - Ishikawa Town * [https://www.minyu-net.com/serial/f-onsen/FM20180429-265897.php Fukushima Onsen Exploration: Neko‑naki Onsen] - {{ill|Fukushima Minyu Newspaper|de|Fukushima Minyu Zeitung|ja|福島民友新聞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Minyu Newspaper|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福島民友|1=Fukushima Minyu Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593163}} {{Onsen-stub}} Nekodaki Onsen is a radioactive spring {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|猫啼温泉|version=100778076|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Radioactive spring]] }} [[ja:猫啼温泉]] [[de:Katzenweinen Onsen]] [[Category:Izumi Shikibu]] [[Category:Ishikawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:Hot springs of Fukushima Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11572001}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:猫啼温泉]] [[ja:猫啼温泉]] [[ja:猫啼温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572001]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Neko-naki Onsen |image = 猫啼温泉郷看板 2012.jpg |Comment = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300}} |Location = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]][[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District]][[:en:Ishikawa, Fukushima|Ishikawa Town]] |Access = By car: from [[:en:Abukuma Kōgen Road|Abukuma Kogen Road]] and {{ill|Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|de|Tamagawa Anschlussstelle (Fukushima)|ja|玉川インターチェンジ (福島県)|lt=Tamagawa IC|lt_ja=玉川IC|lt_de=Tamagawa IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|1=Tamagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572547}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]] for approximately 20 minutes, and from [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|de|Sukagawa Anschlussstelle|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|lt=Sukagawa IC|lt_ja=須賀川IC|lt_de=Sukagawa IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]] for approximately 40 minutes.
By train: approximately a 15‑minute walk from [[:en:Suigun Line|Mizugun Line]] [[:en:Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|Ishikawa Station]]. |Spring Quality = {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|de|Radioaktive Quelle|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} |Temperature = 8°C |Discharge = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Guest Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = |Special Notes = }} '''Neko-naki Onsen''' (猫啼温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District]], [[:en:Ishikawa, Fukushima|Ishikawa Town]] (formerly part of the old [[:en:Provinces of Japan|State]] [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]; and since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era, part of the old [[:en:Iwaki Province (1868)|Iwaki Province]]). == Water Quality == * Simple weak radioactive cold mineral spring ** [[:en:Quell|Spring Mouth]][[:en:Temperature|Temperature]]8.0°C == Hot Spring Area == Although there is no [[:en:Spa town|Onsen town]], there are two [[:en:Ryokan|Inn]]s located in the basin of {{ill|Imadegawa (Fukushima)|de|Imadegawa (Fukushima)|ja|今出川 (福島県)|lt=Imadegawa|lt_ja=今出川|lt_de=Imadegawa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imadegawa (Fukushima)|1=Imade River (Fukushima)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q83237244}}. Novels set in the local inns have also been written, such as [[:en:Yasuo Uchida|Yasuo Uchida]]’s “Thirteen Tombstones” and [[:en:Funahashi Seiichi|Seiichi Funahashi]]’s “A Certain Woman’s Distant View”. == History == This hot spring is said to be associated with the mid‑Heian period female {{ill|Poet|de|Dichter|ja|歌人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poet|1=waka poet|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11122954}}, [[:en:Izumi Shikibu|Izumi Shikibu]]. According to the {{ill|Legend of the Spring’s Opening|de|Legende der Quelle|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Legend of the Spring’s Opening|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, when Izumi Shikibu was en route to Kyoto, she left her [[:en:Cat|Beloved Cat]] behind. It is said that the abandoned cat continued to cry in search of its master, Izumi Shikibu. Consequently, the hot spring came to be known as Neko‑naki Onsen. Later, although the cat fell ill, it regained its vitality after soaking in the hot spring. This discovery of the mineral spring’s healing properties by the locals led to the development of the area as a spa resort. == Access == * By car: approximately 20 minutes via [[:en:Abukuma Kōgen Road|Abukuma Kogen Road]] and {{ill|Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|de|Tamagawa Anschlussstelle (Fukushima)|ja|玉川インターチェンジ (福島県)|lt=Tamagawa IC|lt_ja=玉川IC|lt_de=Tamagawa IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|1=Tamagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572547}} from [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]], and approximately 40 minutes via [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|de|Sukagawa Anschlussstelle|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|lt=Sukagawa IC|lt_ja=須賀川IC|lt_de=Sukagawa IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} from [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]]. * By train: approximately a 15‑minute walk from [[:en:Suigun Line|Mizugun Line]] [[:en:Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|Ishikawa Station]]. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Ishikawa Town ** {{ill|Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|de|Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|ja|片倉温泉 (福島県)|lt=Katakura Onsen|lt_ja=片倉温泉|lt_de=Katakura Onsen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|1=Katakura Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569535}} ** {{ill|Haha-bata Onsen|de|Haha-bata Onsen|ja|母畑温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haha-bata Onsen|1=Bobata Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547236}} ** {{ill|Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|de|Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|ja|塩ノ沢温泉 (福島県)|lt=Shionosawa Onsen|lt_ja=塩ノ沢温泉|lt_de=Shionosawa Onsen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://navi.town.ishikawa.fukushima.jp/blog/entry/003989.html Tour the four hot spring resorts of Ishikawa Town and enjoy the renowned “spa treatment” experience.] - Ishikawa Town * [https://www.minyu-net.com/serial/f-onsen/FM20180429-265897.php Fukushima Onsen Exploration: Neko‑naki Onsen] - {{ill|Fukushima Minyu Newspaper|de|Fukushima Minyu Zeitung|ja|福島民友新聞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Minyu Newspaper|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福島民友|1=Fukushima Minyu Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593163}} {{Onsen-stub}} Nekodaki Onsen is a radioactive spring {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|猫啼温泉|version=100778076|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:猫啼温泉]] [[de:Katzenweinen Onsen]] {{wikidata link|Q11572001}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Hot springs of Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Ishikawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:Izumi Shikibu]] [[Category:Radioactive spring]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

By train: approximately a 15‑minute walk from [[:en:Suigun Line|Mizugun Line]] [[:en:Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|Ishikawa Station]]. |Spring Quality = {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|de|Radioaktive Quelle|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} |Temperature = 8°C |Discharge = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Guest Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = |Special Notes = }} '''Neko-naki Onsen''' (猫啼温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District]], [[:en:Ishikawa, Fukushima|Ishikawa Town]] (formerly part of the old [[:en:Provinces of Japan|State]] [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]; and since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era, part of the old [[:en:Iwaki Province (1868)|Iwaki Province]]). == Water Quality == * Simple weak radioactive cold mineral spring ** [[:en:Quell|Spring Mouth]][[:en:Temperature|Temperature]]8.0°C == Hot Spring Area == Although there is no [[:en:Spa town|Onsen town]], there are two [[:en:Ryokan|Inn]]s located in the basin of {{ill|Imadegawa (Fukushima)|de|Imadegawa (Fukushima)|ja|今出川 (福島県)|lt=Imadegawa|lt_ja=今出川|lt_de=Imadegawa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imadegawa (Fukushima)|1=Imade River (Fukushima)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q83237244}}. Novels set in the local inns have also been written, such as [[:en:Yasuo Uchida|Yasuo Uchida]]’s “Thirteen Tombstones” and [[:en:Funahashi Seiichi|Seiichi Funahashi]]’s “A Certain Woman’s Distant View”. == History == This hot spring is said to be associated with the mid‑Heian period female {{ill|Poet|de|Dichter|ja|歌人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poet|1=waka poet|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11122954}}, [[:en:Izumi Shikibu|Izumi Shikibu]]. According to the {{ill|Legend of the Spring’s Opening|de|Legende der Quelle|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Legend of the Spring’s Opening|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, when Izumi Shikibu was en route to Kyoto, she left her [[:en:Cat|Beloved Cat]] behind. It is said that the abandoned cat continued to cry in search of its master, Izumi Shikibu. Consequently, the hot spring came to be known as Neko‑naki Onsen. Later, although the cat fell ill, it regained its vitality after soaking in the hot spring. This discovery of the mineral spring’s healing properties by the locals led to the development of the area as a spa resort. == Access == * By car: approximately 20 minutes via [[:en:Abukuma Kōgen Road|Abukuma Kogen Road]] and {{ill|Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|de|Tamagawa Anschlussstelle (Fukushima)|ja|玉川インターチェンジ (福島県)|lt=Tamagawa IC|lt_ja=玉川IC|lt_de=Tamagawa IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|1=Tamagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572547}} from [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]], and approximately 40 minutes via [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|de|Sukagawa Anschlussstelle|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|lt=Sukagawa IC|lt_ja=須賀川IC|lt_de=Sukagawa IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} from [[:en:Japan National Route 118|National Route 118]]. * By train: approximately a 15‑minute walk from [[:en:Suigun Line|Mizugun Line]] [[:en:Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|Ishikawa Station]]. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Ishikawa Town ** {{ill|Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|de|Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|ja|片倉温泉 (福島県)|lt=Katakura Onsen|lt_ja=片倉温泉|lt_de=Katakura Onsen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakura Onsen (Fukushima)|1=Katakura Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569535}} ** {{ill|Haha-bata Onsen|de|Haha-bata Onsen|ja|母畑温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haha-bata Onsen|1=Bobata Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547236}} ** {{ill|Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|de|Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|ja|塩ノ沢温泉 (福島県)|lt=Shionosawa Onsen|lt_ja=塩ノ沢温泉|lt_de=Shionosawa Onsen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shionosawa Onsen (Fukushima)|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://navi.town.ishikawa.fukushima.jp/blog/entry/003989.html Tour the four hot spring resorts of Ishikawa Town and enjoy the renowned “spa treatment” experience.] - Ishikawa Town * [https://www.minyu-net.com/serial/f-onsen/FM20180429-265897.php Fukushima Onsen Exploration: Neko‑naki Onsen] - {{ill|Fukushima Minyu Newspaper|de|Fukushima Minyu Zeitung|ja|福島民友新聞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Minyu Newspaper|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福島民友|1=Fukushima Minyu Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593163}} {{Onsen-stub}} Nekodaki Onsen is a radioactive spring {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|猫啼温泉|version=100778076|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:猫啼温泉]] [[de:Katzenweinen Onsen]] {{wikidata link|Q11572001}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Hot springs of Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Ishikawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:Izumi Shikibu]] [[Category:Radioactive spring]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:猫啼温泉]] [[ja:猫啼温泉]] [[ja:猫啼温泉]] {{infobox Onsen |Name = Neko-naki Onsen |image = 猫啼温泉郷看板 2012.jpg |Comment = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300}} |Location = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|en|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|WD=Q1155869|lt=Ishikawa District}}{{ill|Ishikawa, Fukushima|en|Ishikawa, Fukushima|WD=Q1205335|lt=Ishikawa Town}} |Access = By car: from {{ill|Abukuma Kōgen Road|en|Abukuma Kōgen Road|WD=Q11258237|lt=Abukuma Kogen Road}} and {{ill|Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|de|Tamagawa Anschlussstelle (Fukushima)|ja|玉川インターチェンジ (福島県)|lt=Tamagawa IC|lt_ja=玉川IC|lt_de=Tamagawa IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|1=Tamagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572547}} via {{ill|Japan National Route 118|en|Japan National Route 118|WD=Q6156739|lt=National Route 118}} for approximately 20 minutes, and from {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tohoku Expressway}} and {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|de|Sukagawa Anschlussstelle|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|lt=Sukagawa IC|lt_ja=須賀川IC|lt_de=Sukagawa IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} via {{ill|Japan National Route 118|en|Japan National Route 118|WD=Q6156739|lt=National Route 118}} for approximately 40 minutes.
By train: approximately a 15‑minute walk from {{ill|Suigun Line|en|Suigun Line|WD=Q1056202|lt=Mizugun Line}} {{ill|Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|en|Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|WD=Q11587873|lt=Ishikawa Station}}. |Spring Quality = {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|de|Radioaktive Quelle|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} |Temperature = 8°C |Discharge = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Guest Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = |Special Notes = }} '''Neko-naki Onsen''' (猫啼温泉) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|en|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|WD=Q1155869|lt=Ishikawa District}}, {{ill|Ishikawa, Fukushima|en|Ishikawa, Fukushima|WD=Q1205335|lt=Ishikawa Town}} (formerly part of the old {{ill|Provinces of Japan|en|Provinces of Japan|WD=Q860290|lt=State}} {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province}}; and since the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} era, part of the old {{ill|Iwaki Province (1868)|en|Iwaki Province (1868)|WD=Q201583|lt=Iwaki Province}}). == Water Quality == * Simple weak radioactive cold mineral spring ** {{ill|Quell|en|Quell|WD=Q20759607|lt=Spring Mouth}}{{ill|Temperature|en|Temperature|WD=Q11466|lt=Temperature}}8.0°C == Hot Spring Area == Although there is no {{ill|Spa town|en|Spa town|WD=Q4946461|lt=Onsen town}}, there are two {{ill|Ryokan|en|Ryokan|WD=Q562930|lt=Inn}}s located in the basin of {{ill|Imadegawa (Fukushima)|de|Imadegawa (Fukushima)|ja|今出川 (福島県)|lt=Imadegawa|lt_ja=今出川|lt_de=Imadegawa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imadegawa (Fukushima)|1=Imade River (Fukushima)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q83237244}}. Novels set in the local inns have also been written, such as {{ill|Yasuo Uchida|en|Yasuo Uchida|WD=Q10948190|lt=Yasuo Uchida}}’s “Thirteen Tombstones” and {{ill|Funahashi Seiichi|en|Funahashi Seiichi|WD=Q1474002|lt=Seiichi Funahashi}}’s “A Certain Woman’s Distant View”. == History == This hot spring is said to be associated with the mid‑Heian period female {{ill|Poet|de|Dichter|ja|歌人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poet|1=waka poet|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11122954}}, {{ill|Izumi Shikibu|en|Izumi Shikibu|WD=Q239898|lt=Izumi Shikibu}}. According to the {{ill|Legend of the Spring’s Opening|de|Legende der Quelle|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Legend of the Spring’s Opening|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, when Izumi Shikibu was en route to Kyoto, she left her {{ill|Cat|en|Cat|WD=Q146|lt=Beloved Cat}} behind. It is said that the abandoned cat continued to cry in search of its master, Izumi Shikibu. Consequently, the hot spring came to be known as Neko‑naki Onsen. Later, although the cat fell ill, it regained its vitality after soaking in the hot spring. This discovery of the mineral spring’s healing properties by the locals led to the development of the area as a spa resort. == Access == * By car: approximately 20 minutes via {{ill|Abukuma Kōgen Road|en|Abukuma Kōgen Road|WD=Q11258237|lt=Abukuma Kogen Road}} and {{ill|Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|de|Tamagawa Anschlussstelle (Fukushima)|ja|玉川インターチェンジ (福島県)|lt=Tamagawa IC|lt_ja=玉川IC|lt_de=Tamagawa IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Interchange (Fukushima)|1=Tamagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572547}} from {{ill|Japan National Route 118|en|Japan National Route 118|WD=Q6156739|lt=National Route 118}}, and approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tohoku Expressway}} and {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|de|Sukagawa Anschlussstelle|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|lt=Sukagawa IC|lt_ja=須賀川IC|lt_de=Sukagawa IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} from {{ill|Japan National Route 118|en|Japan National Route 118|WD=Q6156739|lt=National Route 118}}. * By train: approximately a
